description: The one strict number-parsing rule accepts a JSON number or a plain decimal string as-is, and rejects every other shape (leading plus, exponent notation, boolean, empty string, thousands separators) as not-a-number, failing closed rather than coercing.

results.reason: leading + is not in the accepted grammar
exponent notation is not in the accepted grammar
not a JSON number and not a string
does not match the accepted grammar
thousands separators are not in the accepted grammar
leading/trailing whitespace is not in the accepted grammar

expected.not_a_number_trigger_behavior: treated as not satisfying either bound on every such observation, never an evaluation error
